mappings.rationaleAI: Transformation of raw materials into finished goods spanning aerospace, semiconductors, and steel corresponds broadly to principles of organization (A), major manufacturing industries (B), fabrication industries (C), and processing industries (D).
Items worn on the human body made from textiles correspond to the clothing and footwear industry (B.3).
Pioneered mass automobile production through the moving assembly line, directly relevant to the automotive industry (B.2).
Fibre-based materials produced by weaving, knitting, and bonding correspond to the textile industry (C.1).
Creation and wearing of clothing, footwear, and accessories as a global industry corresponds to the clothing and footwear industry (B.3).
The transition to mechanized factory manufacturing drove the development of the textile industry (C.1) and the steel industry (C.2), transforming principles of production organization (A).
Paired footwear items as a $200 billion global industry correspond to the clothing and footwear industry (B.3).
Intangible economic outputs including personal care and professional services correspond to the service industries (F).
Design and manufacture of mechanical systems including vehicles and machinery corresponds to the aerospace industry (B.1) and the automotive industry (B.2).
Synthetic polymer materials defined by plasticity correspond to the plastics industry (D.5).
Exploiting living organisms for pharmaceuticals and GMO crops corresponds to the pharmaceuticals industry (D.4).
Machines as systems applying forces to perform work are central to principles of organization of work and production (A).
Engines converting energy into mechanical energy are central to the aerospace industry (B.1) and the automotive industry (B.2).
Robots in manufacturing applications connect to the electronics industry (C.6) and production automation.
Automobiles as four-wheeled motor vehicles directly correspond to the automotive industry (B.2).
Full lifecycle of delivering built assets corresponds to the construction industries (E).
Synthesis of non-carbon-based compounds including catalysts, pigments, and agrochemicals connects to the chemical industry (D.1).
Acceleration of chemical reactions underpinning ~90% of commercial chemical products connects to the chemical industry (D.1).
Petroleum, coal, and natural gas refined into fuels and feedstocks correspond to the petroleum industry (D.2).
Industrial production of oxygen corresponds to production of industrial and residential gases (D.10).
Industrial CO2 applications including food carbonation and fire suppression correspond to production of industrial gases (D.10).
Iron-carbon alloy production at 1.6+ billion tons annually directly corresponds to the steel industry (C.2).
Established the paradigm of industrial research laboratories, directly corresponding to industrial research and development (I).
Human labor as a primary factor of production corresponds to principles of organization of work and production (A).
Final-stage sale of goods directly to consumers corresponds to merchandising and marketing of consumer goods (H).
Tools extending human ability for manufacturing tasks correspond to the tool and die industry (C.7).
Pharmaceutical drugs used to diagnose, cure, and treat disease correspond to the pharmaceuticals industry (D.4).
